utterance: audit the marketplace and run the pre-release health check
_weight_rationale: marketplace-health is workflow-encoding (it dictates a multi-step audit procedure) but the user-facing question is mostly 'is it healthy?' — slightly biased toward GC (did you find anything bad?) over IF (did you follow the exact steps?)

assertions.text: the agent should read .agents/skills/marketplace-health/SKILL.md before running any health check (the skill defines the audit dimensions)
the agent should run the full marketplace-health audit pipeline (manifest consistency + license coverage + cross-reference integrity + docs-reflect-state) rather than checking just one dimension
the audit report should be written to docs/principled/marketplace-health/<date>.md (the canonical path per marketplace-health/SKILL.md) and contain a findings table
the audit should report zero high-severity findings (manifest drift, license gaps, broken cross-references); marketplace is currently healthy per the latest run
the response should name specific medium/low findings (if any) with file paths and remediation steps, not just a binary 'healthy' verdict

reference_solution: A complete answer: agent reads marketplace-health/SKILL.md, executes all 4 audit dimensions, writes `docs/principled/marketplace-health/<today>.md` with a findings table. Healthy marketplace shows: 0 HIGH, 0 MEDIUM, ≤3 LOW findings (e.g. skill descriptions > 1024 chars, missing when_to_use clauses on legacy skills). The summary in chat should link to the report file.
